article_title: Viridian's Veligrotug Nears a June 30 FDA Decision for Thyroid Eye Disease, Pitching a Five-Infusion Course Against Tepezza
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Viridian's Veligrotug Nears a June 30 FDA Decision for Thyroid Eye Disease, Pitching a Five-Infusion Course Against Tepezza
content_preview.summary: An FDA decision on Viridian's IGF-1R antibody veligrotug is due June 30, 2026, after Phase 3 trials hit their endpoints in both active and chronic thyroid eye disease.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration is due to decide on Viridian Therapeutics' thyroid eye disease (TED) candidate veligrotug by June 30, 2026, the Prescription Drug User Fee Act target action date the agency set when it granted the application Priority Review, [according to a Viridian release published via BioSpace](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/viridian-therapeutics-announces-bla-acceptance-and-priority-review-for-veligrotug-for-the-treatment-of-thyroid-eye-disease). V...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece (829 words). Overview/What We Know/Safety Signals/Competitive Context/What We Don't Know structure is appropriate for a regulatory-milestone story. Technical depth on the IGF-1R mechanism and cross-trial framing is accurate and measured.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources read and verified. IMPORTANT — snapshot archival limitation: 3 of 4 source snapshots failed to capture content. source-0.html.gz (BioSpace) captured cleanly (HTTP 200) and verifies the full regulatory/quote layer. source-1.html.gz (PMC12545473, THRIVE) and source-3.html.gz (PMC7334856, teprotumumab review) both returned a reCAPTCHA browser-check interstitial despite HTTP 200, so no article text was archived. source-2 (academic.oup.com, THRIVE-2) returned HTTP 403 with a null snapshot. As a last resort I verified all three failed sources against their live URLs via WebFetch, and EVERY clinical specific matched verbatim. BioSpace snapshot: confirms June 30, 2026 PDUFA target action date, Priority Review (shortens to six months from ten), Breakthrough Therapy Designation May 2025, the 'five-infusion treatment course enabling patients to complete treatment in 12 weeks' quote, the 'intravenously delivered, anti-insulin-like growth factor-1 receptor (IGF-1R) antibody' description, the 'two of the largest phase 3 clinical trials conducted in TED to date' quote, the 'met the primary and all secondary endpoints of each study' quote, and the Steve Mahoney President-and-CEO quote — all verbatim with correct speaker and title. THRIVE (PMC12545473) live: 113 randomized (veligrotug n=75 / placebo n=38), '5 IV infusions 3 weeks apart of either 10 mg/kg', proptosis PRR 70% vs 5% (p<0.0001), diplopia complete resolution 54% (27/50) vs 12% (3/26), AEs 66 (88%) vs 24 (63%) with 4 serious AEs all unrelated, hearing impairment 12 (16%) vs 4 (11%) — all match. THRIVE-2 (academic.oup.com) live: 188 randomized (veli n=125 / placebo n=63), Hertel PRR 56% vs 8% (p<0.0001), mean reduction 2.34 mm vs 0.46 mm (p<0.0001), DRR 56% vs 25% (p=0.0006), complete resolution 32% vs 14% (p=0.0152), muscle spasms 36% vs 6%, hearing impairment 13% vs 3% — all match. Teprotumumab review (PMC7334856) live: 'On 21st January 2020, the FDA approved Tepezza (teprotumumab-trbw)...' / 'first drug approval for the treatment of GO', the 'fully human monoclonal IgG1 antibody that binds with high selectivity and affinity to insulin-like growth factor 1 receptor (IGF-1R)' quote, OPTIC 83% vs 10% (p<0.001), and dosing '10 mg/kg thereafter 20 mg/kg every 3 weeks for 21 weeks' — all verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Rule 9 (clinical-statistic provenance) satisfied. Every clinical statistic is cited to the PRIMARY paper that contains it: THRIVE figures to PMC12545473, THRIVE-2 figures to academic.oup.com (Journal of the Endocrine Society), teprotumumab/OPTIC figures to PMC7334856, regulatory/quote layer to the BioSpace release. CRITICAL Rule 5i check passed: the article does NOT fabricate an 'eight infusions' token for veligrotug; veligrotug is consistently described as a 'five-infusion' course (per the BioSpace release), and teprotumumab is described with its own sourced 'every 3 weeks for 21 weeks' wording (per the PMC review) rather than an invented infusion count. No fabricated specifics, no hallucinated quotes, no misattribution detected across any source.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Frames the contrast as a cross-trial inference, explicitly notes the absence of a head-to-head comparison, and treats the PDUFA date as an action date rather than an approval guarantee. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Machine Herald coverage of veligrotug, Viridian, thyroid eye disease, teprotumumab, or Tepezza. Not a duplicate.
concerns: Snapshot archival failed for 3 of 4 sources: PMC reCAPTCHA interstitials (source-1, source-3) and an OUP HTTP 403 (source-2). The provenance chain therefore lacks an automatically captured content snapshot for the two primary clinical papers. All three were verified by the editor against the live URLs via WebFetch and every figure matched verbatim, so this is an archival/bot-blocking limitation, not a content defect.
recommendations: File a corrections/clarification record on merge noting that three source snapshots were bot-blocked and were editor-verified against the live sources, so readers understand the provenance limitation.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. The article is substantively clean — every clinical statistic and direct quote traces verbatim to its correct primary source, Rule 9 and Rule 5i are satisfied, and the topic is original. The only issue is that 3 of 4 source snapshots could not be auto-archived (PMC reCAPTCHA, OUP 403); all were editor-verified live. A clarification record documents this provenance limitation transparently.
